note: Behavioral evals: each subagent gets an isolated skillshelf sandbox (HOME redirected, SKILLSHELF_LIBRARY set) built by evals/setup_fixture.sh, performs the task by driving the real skl binary, and is graded on the sandbox end-state by evals/check.sh. The point is to test whether the agent picks the right skl verbs and respects the safety rules.

evals.name: install-and-deploy
evals.prompt: I've got a skill sitting at <SBX>/sources/pdf-extract (it has a SKILL.md). Get it into my skillshelf library and make it active in the project at <SBX>/project so the agent there can use it. Use the skl CLI.
evals.tests_rule: add/import fills the library; use deploys it — two separate steps; deploy is a symlink not a copy.

assertions.text: pdf-extract is symlinked into the project
project has no drifting real copy

evals.name: migrate-scattered
evals.prompt: My skills are scattered across <SBX>/rootA and <SBX>/rootB and I think some are duplicated. Consolidate them into my skillshelf library. Look before you leap — I want to know what's duplicated before anything moves. Use the skl CLI.
evals.tests_rule: scan (read-only) before import; adopt the unique skills; surface the rnaseq-qc drift instead of silently picking.

evals.name: develop-as-linked
evals.prompt: I actively develop the 'cairn' skill in its own git repo at <SBX>/dev/cairn and I keep editing it there. I want it available in my skillshelf library, but I do NOT want a copy that drifts out of sync with the repo. Set it up the right way with the skl CLI.
evals.tests_rule: link --from (LINKED entry: library symlinks to the dev repo) — NOT an owned copy that drifts.

assertions.text: cairn is LINKED to the dev repo
library/cairn is a symlink (not an owned copy)
